title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. tenuis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. tenuis, commonly known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herbaceous flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Lady's Mantle have been found to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Lady's Mantle is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used topically to aid in skin care and wound healing.
